body:value: Saving represents the excess of current income over current expenditure of various sectors of the economy. It is the balancing item on the income and outlay accounts of the producing enterprises, households, Government administration and other final consumers. Estimates of domestic saving are prepared at current prices only by type of institutions i.e., public sector, private corporate sector and household sector (residual), separately. Data contains Gross Domestic Savings, Net domestic savings, Net capital inflow and Finances for gross capital formation.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: National Product and Related Aggregates
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation
field_sector:name: Finance
Economy
Statistics
title: Domestic Saving by type of Institution at Current Prices

body:value: DRDO attracted attention of the parliamentarians throughout the year. Member of Parliament from both houses raised questions on subject matters such as BrahMos missile, Kaveri engine, Low Intensity Conflict (LIC), Light Combat Aircraft (LCA), Missile Development programmes, Performance of DRDO, Self-reliance in Defence R&D , Exodus of Scientists from DRDO and Main Battle Tank (MBT) Arjun.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: DRDO ANNUAL REPORT
field_ministry_department:name: Ministry of Defence
Department of Defence Research and Development
Defence Research and Development Organisation (DRDO)
field_sector:name: Defence
title: DRDO in Parliament

body:value: The data refers to information on the drinking water facility and source of water in the selected sample states. It provides details of Availability of Drinking Water (%age) and Source of Drinking Water (Well, Tap or other). The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission which covered 48 districts and 480 schools across 17 states of India for 'Performance Evaluation Report of Cooked Mid-Day Meal (CMDM), 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2000 to 2006.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Education
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Education
Elementary
title: Drinking Water Facility and Source of Water - Based on Sample Survey

Elementary
title: Dropout Rates

body:value: The data refers to Dropouts and Out of School Children in urban Slums. It provides details of households with out of school children, number of dropouts or out of school child, number of dropouts or out of school children belonging to - SC/ST, OBC or General, female dropouts or out of school children and percentage of schools having pre primary sections. The outcome is based on the sample survey undertaken by Programme Evaluation Organisation (PEO), Planning commission in eleven states and two union territories for 'Evaluation Report On Sarva Shiksha Abhiyan, 2010'. The reference period for the study was 2003 to 2007.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Education
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Education
Elementary
title: Dropouts and Out of School Children in Slums - Based on Sample Survey

body:value:
Get data of committee-wise reports presented to Rajya Sabha. It gives name of the committee, type of the committee, report number, report title, date of presentation and link to open the PDF file of the report.
The Rajya Sabha or Council of States is the upper house of the Parliament of India. Members sit for six-year terms, with one third of the members retiring every two years.The Rajya Sabha meets in continuous sessions and, unlike the Lok Sabha, the lower house of Parliament, is not subject to dissolution.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Parliamentary Committee Reports (Rajya Sabha)

body:value: The data refers to the Community Leaders contribution regarding the various activities. It is believed that community workers should play a very important role in the success of an AWC. They could help in the following activities; selection of location, space, getting beneficiaries, helping in ensuring adequate infrastructure for an AWC and other activities like solving problems of beneficiaries and motivating people to avail the services of this facility etc.. Community leaders contribution for almost all of the activities was good in Kerala.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Health
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Health and Family welfare
Health
title: Community Leaders Contribution regarding various activities

body:value: The percentage of BPL population in the urban areas in India has declined from 23.64% to 21.7% in 2004-05 (based on mixed recall period). A comparison of poverty estimates based on mixed recall period is given in table.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Housing and Urban Affairs
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Urban
Development
title: Comparison Of Poverty Estimates Based On Mixed Recall Period - Eleventh Five Year Plan Period

body:value: The percentage of BPL population in the urban areas in India has declined from 32.4% in 199394 to 25.7% in 2004-05 (based on uniform recall period). The NSSO 61st Round shows that urban poverty has registered a decline in percentage terms; however, it has increased in absolute terms by 4.4 million persons. The fact is that the number of urban poor is rising continuously since 197374 as per the uniform recall period. A comparison of poverty estimates based on uniform recall period is given in table.
field_asset_jurisdiction:name: All India
field_group_name:name: Housing and Urban Affairs
field_ministry_department:name: NITI Aayog
field_sector:name: Urban
Rural
Development
title: Comparison Of Poverty Estimates Based On Uniform Recall Period
